name: Guinea-Bissau Portuguese

ancestors.notes: European Portuguese colonial base. Guinea-Bissau was a Portuguese colony until 1974.

Guinea-Bissau Creole (Kriol/GBC) adstrate: the true lingua franca, spoken as L1 by ~44% and as L2 by most of the population. Descended from Portuguese; exerts strong influence on the local Portuguese register.
Fula (Fulah) substrate: one of the major languages of Guinea-Bissau (Pulaar-speaking communities in the east); phonological transfer into Portuguese.

notes: Guinea-Bissau Portuguese (Português da Guiné-Bissau). Official language; spoken as L1 by a small educated elite (<10%); Guinea-Bissau Creole (Kriol) is the actual national lingua franca. Portuguese functions mainly in formal, governmental, and educational domains. KEY FEATURES: (1) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM: /ɨ/ absent; full articulation of unstressed vowels. (2) SIBILANTS: alveolar [s] in coda (no palatalisation). (3) RHOTIC: alveolar trill [r]. (4) CREOLE TRANSFER: strong lexical and syntactic influence from Kriol; many speakers are effectively L2 users of Portuguese. (5) Very limited documentation of phonological features. Sources: Couto (1994), Kihm (1994), PALOP linguistic studies.

sources.author: Couto, H.H. do
sources.title: O crioulo português da Guiné-Bissau
sources.notes: Key reference for Guinea-Bissau Creole and its Portuguese relationship
